subject: Re: Warp/M <--> Relion
body:
Hi Jesse,
Based on your recent messages you’ve been doing lots of ‘custom workflows’ - given that Warp/M appears to be working well under normal circumstances I’d suggest there is likely some user error 🤷‍♂️
What does your particle export command look like?
You could generate 3D subtomos from the same particles in both packages and check that they reconstruct correctly with relion_reconstruct as a sanity check. This would exclude the variability of the 3D refinement…
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Oct 9, 2024, at 01:19, Jesse Hansen <bluufive@gmail.com> wrote:
﻿WarpTools/2.0.0dev28 and Relion 5.0 beta3 commit 12cf15.
I imported tilt series alignments into Relion and Warp. I generate tomograms and they are perfectly aligned with one another.  However, I cannot successfully move particles between RELION and Warp/M.
1) Particles extracted in Relion refines well to 7A.  I import into M, run first round of refinement (iter 0) and it produces a blob at 50A.  
2) Same particles as above are instead extracted in Warp. Run a refinement in RELION and it gets stuck at 30A and overall is poor quality map.
any thoughts are welcome.
J
